original: Dmitri Shostakovich completes his 7th Symphony in Siberia
Apollo 8 returns to Earth
Musical revue "Thumbs Up", including music and lyrics by Vernon Duke, James Hanley, and Ira Gershwin(among others), premieres at the St. James Theatre, NYC; runs for 156 performance
Edmonton OilerWayne Gretzkybecomes fastest NHLer to get 100 point in a season (his 38th game), with 4 goals and an assist in 10-3 win over visiting Los Angeles Kings
Red Sox owner Harry Frazee announces they will deal any player except Harry Hooper, Hooper is sent to the White Sox after 1920 season
Stage play "Peter Pan" by J. M. Barrie premieres at the Duke of York Theatre in London
HMS Beagle with Charles Darwin on board departs England for a survey of South America, a voyage Darwin later said "determined my whole career"
1st appearance ofThe Supremeson "TheEd SullivanShow", singing "Come See About Me"

Pittsburgh Penguins center and part-ownerMario Lemieuxscores a goal and 2 assists in his return to the NHL after more than 3 years out with injury problems and lingering cancer issues; Penguins beat Toronto Maple Leafs, 5-0
Luka Dončićbecomes the first player to register a 60-21-10 triple-double in NBA history as the Dallas Mavericks beat the NY Knicks, 126-121; first 60-point game in Mavs franchise history
George Cayley, English inventor who founded science of aerodynamics (built 1st glider to carry a person), born in Scarborough, England (d. 1854)
James Harrison, Australian blood and plasma donor known as 'the man with the golden arm' whose donations protected over 2 million babies, born in Junee, New South Wales (d. 2025)
Cole Hamels, American baseball pitcher (MLB All-Star 2007, 11, 12, 16; World Series MVP & NLCS MVP 2008 Philadelphia Phillies; pitched no-hitter 2015 Texas Rangers), born in San Diego, California
Bright Vachirawit [Vachirawit Chivaaree], Thai actor and singer (2gether franchise), born in Nakhon Chai Si, Thailand
NASA unveils plans to capture a 500 ton asteroid in 2025
1st American skimobiles (North Conway, NH)

"Chicago" (Best Picture 2003), film based on John Kander and Fred Ebb's musical, directed by Rob Marshall, starring Renée Zellweger, Richard Gere, and Catherine Zeta-Jones is released in NYC, Los Angeles, and select North American cities
(Winfield) Scotty Moore, American guitarist (Elvis Presley; Roy Orbison), born in Gadsden, Tennessee (d. 2016)
Doina Melinte, Romanian athlete (Olympic gold 800m, silver 1500m 1984; World Indoor C'ship gold 1500m 1987, 89), born in Hudești, Romania
Andre Tippett, American NFL Hall of Fame linebacker, 1982-93, 5X Pro Bowl (New England Patriots), born in Birmingham, Alabama
Dana Chladek, Czech-American slalom kayaker (Olympic bronze 1992, silver 1996), born in Děčín, Czech Republic
Bill Goldberg, American pro wrestler (WCW Heavyweight C'ship 1998; WWE Heavyweight C'ship 2003; WWE HOF), born in Tulsa, Oklahoma
Chyna [Joanie Laurer], American professional wrestler and reality TV star, born in Rochester, New York (d. 2016)
Heather O'Rourke, American actress (Happy Days, Poltergeist), born in San Diego, California (d. 1988)
Biddle University beats Livingstone College 5-0 in 1st black college football game, in Salisbury, North Carolina
"Sweet Adeline", a barbershop quartet favorite, with lyrics by Richard Husch Gerard and music by Harry Armstrong, is first sung
Radio City Music Hall, designed by Edward Durell Stone and Donald Deskey opens at Rockerfeller Center in Manhattan, New York City
1st "Howdy Doody Show" (Puppet Playhouse), telecast on NBC
Dow Jones Industrial Average hits record 3792.93
Bill Sharman(Boston) ends NBA free throw streak of 55 games
Ballon d'Or: Borussia Mönchengladbach's Danish forward Allan Simonsen is named Europe's best football player ahead of Hamburg forward Kevin Keegan and Nancy midfielderMichel Platini
Joe Sakicrecords his 1,000th career point, an assist against the St. Louis Blues
Raheem Sterling scores to give Manchester City 1-0 win over Newcastle United at St. James' Park; EPL record 18th consecutive win; streak ends with 0-0 draw at Crystal Palace 31/12
(Willie) "Bunk" Johnson, American trumpeter, traditional jazz revivalist, and raconteur, born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 1949) [he claimed year of birth was 1889]
Larry Osborne, Trinidadian-British army officer and navigator air traffic controller (1st black man to reach rank of Group Captain rank), born in Trinidad (d. 1996)
Charmian Carr, American actress (The Sound of Music), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2016)
Kjeld Kirk Kristiansen, Danish businessman (CEO of Lego Group, 1979-2004), born in Billund, Denmark
Terry Bozzio, American drummer (Missing Persons; Frank Zappa), born in San Francisco, California
Karl Denson, American funk and jazz saxophonist, flutist and vocalist (Lenny Kravitz; Tiny Universe), born in Santa Ana, California
Daniel Levitin, American-Canadian cognitive psychologist, writer (This Is Your Brain on Music: The Science of a Human Obsession), record producer, and musician, born in San Francisco, California
Wilson Cruz [Echevarría], American stage and screen actor (My So Called Life; Party of Five), born in Brooklyn, NYC
Lily Cole, English model and actress, born in Torquay, England
Rick Porcello, American baseball pitcher (World Series 2018, AL Cy Young Award & MLB wins leader 2016 Boston Red Sox), born in Morristown, New Jersey
Olivia Cooke, English actress (House of the Dragon, Ready Player One), born in Oldham, England
Briar Nolet, Canadian dancer and actress (The Next Step; Blood Hunters), born in Toronto, Ontario
13th United Negro College Fund raises $11,000,000
Bengals hire Dave Shula as youngest NFL coach (32)

Edmonton Oilers beat the Calgary Flames, 4-1 to record their 500th all-time NHL victory; set in just 915 games for a record of 500-295-120, good for a winning % of .612; best in history upon reaching milestone
Ether 1st used in childbirth in US in Jefferson, Georgia
Hawaiian Fire Department established
1st youth hostel in US opens (Northfield, Mass)
Bulgaria stops jamming Radio Free Europe after more than 3 decades
Queen Julianaof the Netherlands grants independence to Indonesia
Australian Dennis Lillee becomes the leading wicket-taker in test cricket with 310 wickets
Ballon d'Or: Milan 1-2-3; Dutch striker Marco van Basten wins his 1st of 3 best football player in Europe awards ahead of Ruud Gullit and Frank Rijkaard
Sir John Layton Jarvis, English thoroughbred race horse trainer (9 x British Classic Races; 3 x British flat racing Champion Trainer), born in Newmarket, England (d. 1968)
Oscar Levant, American pianist, composer, and actor (An American in Paris; Dance of Life), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania (d. 1972)
(Mitchell) "Booty" Wood, American jazz trombonist (Lionel Hampton, Erskine Hawkins, Count Basie, Duke Ellington), born in Wedowee, Alabama (d. 1987)
Bruce Hobbs, American jockey (youngest to ride winner of English Grand National on Battleship 1938), born on Long Island, New York (d. 2005)
Jacqueline Fontyn, Belgian pianist, contemporary classical composer (Vent d'Est for Accordion and Strings; Halo for Harp), and music educator (1963-90), born in Antwerp, Belgium
John Amos, American actor (Good Times; Roots; Coming to America; The West Wing), born in Newark, New Jersey (d. 2024)
Mike Heron, Scottish folk-rock singer-songwriter (Incredible String Band, 1965-74, 1999-2006), born in Edinburgh, Scotland
Joan Manuel Serrat, Spanish pop, rock and nueva canción singer-songwriter, born in Barcelona, Spain
Mick Jones, English rock guitarist (Foreigner - "I Want to Know What Love Is"), born in Portsmouth, Hampshire, England
T.S. Monk [Thelonious Sphere Monk III], American jazz drummer, composer, and bandleader, born in New York City
Karla Bonoff, American rock singer-songwriter (Restless Nights; "Someone To Lay Down Beside Me"), born in Santa Monica, California
Ian Gomez, American actor (Cougar Town), born in New York City
Jean-Christophe Boullion, French auto racer (International Formula 3000 Series 1994; European Le Mans Series 2005, 06), born in Saint-Brieuc, France
Carson Palmer, American NFL quarterback (Heisman Trophy 2002, USC; Pro Bowl 2005, 06, 15; Cincinnati Bengals, Arizona Cardinals), born in Fresno, California
Siobhán McSweeney, Irish actress and TV presenter (Derry Girls, The Great Pottery Thrown Down), born in Aherla, Ireland
Metrik [Tom Mundell], British electronic dance music producer and re-mixer ("Freefall"; "Gravity"; "Chasing Sunrise"), born in London, England
Milos Raonic, Canadian tennis player (8 ATP titles), born in Titograd, Montenegro
Steve Largent sets all-time NFL record for career catches when he catches his 752nd pass
Musical "Les Misérables" opens in the US at the Kennedy Center, Washington, D.C.
Foundation Stone of Cathedral of St John laid (NYC)
Ballon d'Or: Manchester United midfielder Bobby Charlton wins award for best European football player; claims award by a single point ahead of Benfica striker Eusébio
Columbia Records releases Leonard Cohen's debut album "Songs of Leonard Cohen"
Jane Wilde, Irish poet, nationalist and mother of Oscar Wilde, born in Wexford (d. 1896)
Pavel Tretyakov, Russian businessman and patron of art, born in Moscow (d. 1897)
Joseph Lauber, Swiss concert pianist and composer, born in Ruswil, Switzerland (d. 1952)
Paul Costello, American rower (Olympic gold double sculls 1920, 24, 28), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1986)
Arch Ward, American sportswriter (Chicago Tribune - proposed baseball's all-star game), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1955)
Conrad L. Raiford, Goodwill Ambassador (d. 2002)
Eddie Wilcox, American jazz pianist, arranger, and bandleader (Jimmie Lunceford Orchestra), born in Method, North Carolina (d. 1968)
Mary Kornman, American actress (Desert Trail, Queen of the Jungle, Swing it Professor), born in Idaho Falls (d. 1973)

(Alan) "Albhy" Galuten, American Grammy Award-winning record producer, composer, and arranger (Bee Gees), born in Hartsdale, New York
Larry Byrom, American rock guitarist (Steppenwolf, 1969-71), born in Huntsville, Alabama
Martin Birch, British sound engineer (Fleetwood Mac; Deep Purple ) and record producer (Whitesnake; Iron Maiden), born in Woking, Surrey, England (d. 2020)
Roberto Bettega, Italian soccer forward (42 caps; Juventus 326 games, 129 goals), born in Turin, Italy
Craig Reynolds, American baseball shortstop who was MLB All Star 1978, 79; Seattle Mariners, Houston Astros, born in Houston, Texas
Tovah Feldshuh, American actress (Idolmaker, Terror out of the Sky), born in New York City
Arthur Kent [The Scud Stud], American newscaster (prominent Gulf War coverage for NBC), born in Alberta, Canada
Steve Jones, American golfer (US Open 1996, 8 PGA Tour titles), born in Artesia, New Mexico
Maryam D'Abo, actress (Living Daylights, Xtro, Until Sept), born in London, England
Mark Few, American basketball coach (Gonzaga University 1999-present; AP Coach of the Year 2017; Naismith Coach of the Year 2017, 21), born in Creswell, Oregon
Sherri Steinhauer, American golfer (du Maurier Classic 1992, British Open 2006), born in Madison, Wisconsin
Timothy Hacker, 10k runner, born in Milwaukee, Wisconsin
